basics.name: Xiaosi Zhang
basics.label: Senior Software Systems Engineer
basics.summary: Senior Software Systems Engineer with a Ph.D. from Vanderbilt University, working at the intersection of semiconductor systems, computer vision, and machine learning. Designs scalable end-to-end solutions for registration, defect detection, and robust matching in structured environments.

work.name: PDF Solutions
work.summary: 1D Registration Location Discovery – Geometry-based Pattern Search (Mar 2026 – Present); Image-based Registration & Template Matching Pipeline (Oct 2025 – Mar 2026)
highlights: Designed a geometry-aware search system to identify robust registration locations from semiconductor layouts using edge-based spatial analysis.
Built efficient candidate generation and filtering mechanisms with spatial indexing and rule-based isolation constraints for structured layout patterns.
Developed scoring strategies to evaluate pattern quality and robustness under highly noisy conditions.
Designed and integrated an end-to-end pipeline from layout parsing to candidate search and result generation, deployed on HPC environments (SGE clusters) for scalable large-scale layout processing.
Designed an end-to-end image-based pipeline for registration location selection, including layout rasterization, candidate sampling, template matching, and quality scoring.
Applied computer vision techniques (template matching and correlation analysis) to identify robust patterns under noise, ambiguity, and repetitive structures.
Developed data-driven quality metrics (e.g., peak ratio, feature coverage) for pattern ranking and selection.
Built scalable and production-ready workflows, deployed across HPC (SGE) and cloud environments (AWS) with debugging and validation pipelines for large-scale semiconductor inspection.

work.name: Vanderbilt University
work.summary: Ph.D. research in optoelectronic characterization, signal processing, and data-driven analysis.
highlights: Performed optoelectronic characterization using scanning photocurrent microscopy (SPCM), generating spatially-resolved signal maps for device behavior and defect patterns.
Analyzed photocurrent and electrical signals to characterize carrier transport, device variability, and pattern-level anomalies across spatial regions.
Developed signal and image processing pipelines (MATLAB/Python) for noise reduction, feature extraction, and spatial pattern recognition, enabling quantitative comparison across devices.
Explored data-driven approaches for pattern analysis and feature extraction from high-dimensional measurement data.

education.institution: Huazhong University of Science and Technology

awards.title: Hamilton Fellowship
awards.summary: Fellowship awarded to support doctoral studies.
